source_jobs.title: Cinematographer/Producer/Director/Editor
source_jobs.description:
To help us stay organized, please apply using the posting only and include a portfolio — it’s the best way to ensure we see your work. We are unable to review applications sent via email, direct messages, or website inquiries, and outreach through these channels may impact consideration.



About Nen Creative

Nen Creative is an interdisciplinary studio helping early-stage startups accelerate their vision through branding, design, and marketing.

Since 2022, we've partnered with some of the world’s top founders and institutions — including Amazon, Pear VC, Andreessen Horowitz, Mercor, Context Inc, Netic, Listen Labs, Tech Week, the University of Pittsburgh, Somethings, Mantis VC, Spur, Flora, and more. We’re growing fast and looking for great people to join us.



About the Opportunity

We’re building the motion studio we always dreamed of — fast-moving, design-led, and trusted by the best in the world. In just over a year, we’ve gone from zero to partnering with Amazon, top-tier VCs, and the most ambitious startups out there.

Now, we’re looking for a versatile cinematographer/producer/director/editor with a strong eye, on-set presence, and storytelling instincts to help create authentic, visually compelling stories for modern brands. Depending on your skills and interests, you may focus on one discipline or work across all — from concept and planning, to shooting and directing, to editing and delivery.

You’ll join a small, fast growing team working across testimonials, light commercial shoots, and brand content — with the opportunity to grow into a larger role as we expand our commercial production arm. This is a chance to help shape the creative direction of projects from start to finish while collaborating closely with clients and our in-house team.



What We’re Looking For

 - Cinematography – A strong eye and visual taste — someone who knows how to frame a shot, light a scene, and bring a story to life.

 - Producing – Confident managing production logistics, coordinating schedules, setting up gear, and keeping shoots running smoothly.

 - Directing – Able to guide non-actors through interviews, direct talent on set, and collaborate with clients to achieve creative goals.

 - Editing – Skilled in post-production workflows, from organizing footage to cutting polished videos with strong pacing, sound, and visual storytelling.



Bonus Points

 - Experience producing, writing, or directing — or interest in growing into a fuller commercial/creative lead role

 - Strong understanding of post workflows, editing, or sound

 - Familiarity with studio shoots, remote capture setups, or creative content for digital platforms

 - Past work in agency, branded content, or fast-paced client environments

source_jobs.title: Brand Designer
source_jobs.description:
Please apply using the form and include a portfolio — it’s the best way to ensure we see your work. We will not review applications sent via email, direct messages, or website inquiries.

This role must be based in the US.




ABOUT NEN CREATIVE

Nen Creative is an interdisciplinary studio helping tech startups launch bold ideas into the world — building the brands, products, and stories that power their growth.

Since 2022, we've partnered with some of the world’s top founders and institutions — including Amazon, Pear VC, Andreessen Horowitz, Mercor, Context Inc, Netic, Listen Labs, Tech Week, and many more. We’re growing crazy fast and are looking for amazing people to join us!




ABOUT THE OPPORTUNITY

We’re building the studio we always wanted to work at — fast-moving, design-led, and trusted by the best in tech. In a short time, we’ve gone from zero to partnering with Amazon, top VCs, and some of the most ambitious startups in the world.

Now we’re looking for a Brand Designer to lead the creation of bold, modern brand identities and websites for high-growth startups. You’ll join a small, focused team where your work has direct impact on how tomorrow’s companies launch and scale.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Lead the development of brand identities from concept through execution, including logos, typography, color systems, and full brand guidelines

 - Design websites in Figma that translate brand strategy into strong digital experiences

 - Create client-facing design assets across pitch decks, marketing collateral, social graphics, launch materials, and other brand touchpoints

 - Extend brand systems across real-world applications to ensure the work feels cohesive beyond the initial identity

 - Present and communicate work directly to clients, helping them make confident creative decisions

 - Balance multiple design explorations with high-level craft and attention to detail

 - Partner with creative directors, strategists, and PMs to ensure design outcomes align with client goals

 - Drive visual consistency across brands, websites, decks, and marketing design projects
   
   


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 2-4 years of experience in brand design (agency or studio preferred)

 - A strong portfolio that showcases:
   
   - Brand identities created from scratch (not just refreshes or marketing visuals)
   
   - Website design that feels modern, clean, and built for tech/startup audiences

 - Confidence in client communication — you can present work, explain rationale, and guide non-designers through decisions

 - Strong grasp of typography, layout, and composition with a modern, tech-forward aesthetic

 - Proficiency in Figma and Adobe Creative Suite

 - Ability to work independently while collaborating with a team in a fast-paced environment
   
   


BONUS POINTS

 - Experience in Webflow or hands-on website building

 - Motion, 3D, or illustration skills

 - Marketing design experience across campaigns, social, or advertising

 - Comfort working in unstructured, high-growth environments
   
   


WHY JOIN NEN CREATIVE

 - Be part of a small, fast-growing studio trusted by world-class startups and investors

 - Work directly with top founders shaping the future of tech

 - Clear growth path as we scale

 - Remote-friendly, flexible, and entrepreneurial environment

 - Healthcare, PTO, 401K

source_jobs.title: Production Coordinator/Junior Producer
source_jobs.description:
We’re currently only reviewing SF-based applicants.



About Nen Creative

Nen Creative partners with tech startups to launch bold ideas into the world, creating the brands, products, and stories that fuel their growth.

Since 2022, we’ve partnered with some of the world’s top founders and institutions, including Amazon, Pear VC, Andreessen Horowitz, Mercor, Context Inc, Netic, Listen Labs, Tech Week, the University of Pittsburgh, Somethings, Mantis VC, Spur, Flora, and more.

We’re growing fast and looking for great people to join us.



Please submit your application through this posting only. We are unable to review applications sent via email, direct messages, or website inquiries, and outreach through these channels may impact consideration.



About the Opportunity

We’re looking for a motivated, detail-oriented Production Coordinator to join our growing team. You’ll work directly under our Producer, supporting the day-to-day logistics of multiple productions at once.

You’ll be in the mix from pre-production through wrap, helping keep everything on track. From time to time, you’ll also jump in as a producer on smaller projects.

This is an ideal role for someone early in their career who is eager to learn, willing to put in the hours, and ready to grow in a fast-moving production environment.

Location: Bay Area (required). This role will include in-person shoot days.



Responsibilities

 - Support the Producer with day-to-day production tasks across multiple projects

 - Keep paperwork, files, and communications organized and moving on schedule

 - Assist with coordinating logistics across pre-production and shoot days (crew, talent, locations, rentals, travel)

 - Communicate with clients, crew, and vendors on behalf of the production team

 - Help build and distribute key production docs (call sheets, schedules, contact lists, releases)

 - Track details across rounds and timelines so nothing slips (deliverables, approvals, shot lists, notes)

 - Support on-set execution: coordination, time management, and real-time problem solving

 - Own file organization and handoffs (footage delivery, folder structure, naming, archiving)

 - Jump in wherever needed to help things run smoothly
   
   

What We’re Looking For

 - Based in the Bay Area (required)

 - Obsessively detail-oriented. Nothing slips through the cracks

 - Highly organized and able to juggle multiple projects at once

 - Calm, resourceful, and dependable under pressure

 - A strong communicator, both written and verbal

 - Proactive. You find solutions before problems become problems

 - A great team player who is easy to work with and takes ownership



Why Join Nen

 - Be part of a small, fast-growing creative studio trusted by world-class startups and investors

 - Work directly with the partners and production leadership, gaining exposure to both creative and business operations

 - Healthcare coverage, PTO, and company holidays

 - Salary range $60,000-80,000

source_jobs.title: Account Manager (Studio)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT NEN CREATIVE

Nen Creative partners with tech startups to launch bold ideas into the world, creating the brands, products, and stories that fuel their growth.

Since 2022, we've partnered with some of the world's top founders and institutions including Amazon, Pear VC, Andreessen Horowitz, Mercor, Context Inc, Netic, Listen Labs, Tech Week, the University of Pittsburgh, Somethings, Mantis VC, Spur, Flora, and more.

We're growing fast and looking for great people to join us.

Please submit your application through this posting only. We are unable to review applications sent via email, direct messages, or website inquiries, and outreach through these channels may impact consideration.




ABOUT THE OPPORTUNITY

We're looking for an Account Manager to be the first point of contact for incoming clients and the connective tissue between them and our creative team. This role sits at the intersection of client relationships and production — you'll guide prospects from initial inquiry through scoping and close, then maintain the relationship to drive repeat business and referrals.

You have a background in film or production, which means you can speak credibly about timelines, deliverables, and creative process from day one. You're organized, personable, and care deeply about the client experience. This is a remote role.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Client Intake & Conversion

 - Own the inbound lead pipeline — respond quickly, qualify opportunities, and guide prospects through the decision process

 - Lead discovery calls to understand client goals, scope projects accurately, and set clear expectations

 - Prepare proposals and scopes of work in collaboration with creative leadership

 - Close new business and hand off seamlessly to the creative producer and production team

Account Management

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for all pricing questions, scope changes, and contract matters

 - Build and nurture client relationships that generate repeat business and referrals

 - Develop and maintain the sales playbook, proposal templates, and client-facing collateral

 - Proactively request client reviews, testimonials, and flag clients who are strong candidates for case studies

CRM & Pipeline

 - Own the health of the CRM — keep pipeline data accurate, up to date, and actionable

 - Track and report on key sales metrics including conversion rates, lead sources, and deal velocity

 - Ensure all client and deal records are maintained accurately in Airtable and our CRM

Production Handoff & Coordination

 - Collaborate with producers and creatives to ensure project scopes are realistic and resourced correctly

 - Flag scope creep or misaligned expectations early and resolve them before they reach the production team

 - Identify process gaps and propose improvements to how we intake, scope, and close clients
   
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 2–4 years of experience in a producer, account management, business development, sales, or growth role — ideally at an agency, production company, or creative studio

 - Background in film, video, or content production is a strong plus — you understand how shoots, edits, and creative timelines actually work

 - Natural communicator — you're warm, clear, and clients feel confident in your hands

 - Highly organized — you can manage multiple active deals and client relationships simultaneously

 - Self-directed and proactive — you surface problems before they become fires

 - Comfortable working independently in a remote environment

 - Strong written English and attention to detail

source_jobs.title: Creative Producer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT NEN CREATIVE

Nen Creative partners with tech startups to launch bold ideas into the world, creating the brands, products, and stories that fuel their growth.

Since 2022, we've partnered with some of the world's top founders and institutions including Amazon, Pear VC, Andreessen Horowitz, Mercor, Context Inc, Netic, Listen Labs, Tech Week, the University of Pittsburgh, Somethings, Mantis VC, Spur, Flora, and more.

We're growing fast and looking for great people to join us.


Please submit your application through this posting only. We are unable to review applications sent via email, direct messages, or website inquiries, and outreach through these channels may impact consideration.




ABOUT THE OPPORTUNITY

We’re looking for a Creative Producer to serve as both a key client-facing partner and a core driver of project execution from start to finish.

This role sits at the intersection of sales, creative strategy, and project leadership. You’ll often be the first point of contact for new opportunities, helping shape scopes, guide clients through the decision-making process, and bring a strong production perspective to early conversations. Once a project kicks off, you’ll remain closely involved as the main point of contact, partnering with our Director of Production and internal creative leads to keep work moving smoothly and clients confident.

We’re looking for someone who can scope creatively, think critically about how work gets made, and lead clients through the full lifecycle of a project with clarity and good judgment.

You understand how ideas move from concept to execution. You know how to evaluate tradeoffs across speed, cost, craft, partners, formats, and outputs. You’re organized, personable, and able to manage multiple stakeholders while protecting both the client experience and the integrity of the work. This is a remote role.




WHAT YOU’LL DO


NEW BUSINESS & SCOPING

 - Serve as the first point of contact for new leads, guiding prospects from inquiry through close

 - Lead discovery conversations to understand client goals, timeline, budget, and creative needs

 - Scope projects with a strong production lens, balancing ambition with feasibility across budget, timing, resourcing, and output

 - Prepare proposals, scopes of work, and pricing in collaboration with leadership and internal creative leads


CLIENT LEADERSHIP & PROJECT MANAGEMENT

 - Act as the primary point of contact for clients from kickoff through final delivery

 - Lead projects end-to-end, keeping timelines, deliverables, communication, and next steps on track

 - Partner closely with the Director of Production and creative team to align on staffing, priorities, and execution

 - Identify risks, efficiencies, and executional opportunities early

 - Manage client expectations, project changes, and multiple stakeholders with clarity and confidence




PROCESS & ACCOUNT GROWTH

 - Build strong client relationships that lead to repeat business and referrals

 - Maintain accurate project and pipeline records across Airtable and related systems

 - Help improve internal processes across intake, scoping, and project management
   
   


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 3 to 6 years of experience in creative production, account management, project management, or client services, ideally within an agency, production company, or creative studio

 - Strong background in film, video, content production, or campaign execution

 - Ability to scope creatively and think strategically about how work gets made

 - Strong judgment around production tradeoffs across budget, timeline, creative ambition, and execution

 - Confident client-facing presence, able to build trust and lead conversations with clarity

 - Excellent project management skills, with the ability to keep multiple projects and stakeholders moving at once

 - Strong communicator, both verbally and in writing

 - Highly organized, proactive, and calm under pressure

 - Comfortable working indepe

source_jobs.title: Associate Agency Producer
source_jobs.description:
Location: SF/LA preferred, Clients are in west coast




ABOUT NEN CREATIVE

Nen Creative partners with tech startups to launch bold ideas into the world, creating the brands, products, and stories that fuel their growth.

Since 2022, we've partnered with some of the world’s top founders and institutions — including Amazon, Pear VC, Andreessen Horowitz, Mercor, Context Inc, Netic, Listen Labs, Tech Week, the University of Pittsburgh, Somethings, Mantis VC, Spur, Flora, and more.

We’re growing fast and looking for great people to join us.



Please submit your application through this posting only. We are unable to review applications sent via email, direct messages, or website inquiries, and outreach through these channels may impact consideration.







ABOUT THE OPPORTUNITY

We’re building the production studio we always wanted to work in — fast, collaborative, and trusted by world-class startups. Our work spans founder stories, commercials, launch videos, and motion-driven campaigns.

We’re looking for an Agency Producer to partner closely with our in-house production team and clients — managing projects from brief to delivery. You’ll be the connective tissue between creative, production, and client teams, ensuring clarity, quality, and on-time execution.

This role combines client service, creative problem-solving, and production oversight.




WHAT YOU’LL DO




PROJECT & PRODUCTION OVERSIGHT

 - Manage multiple video and motion projects from kickoff to final delivery

 - Build and track timelines, budgets, and deliverables across all stakeholders

 - Anticipate risks or scope changes early and align the team accordingly

 - Keep Asana boards, production schedules, and deliverables current

Client & Partner Collaboration

 - Serve as a key client contact throughout projects

 - Ensure client expectations are clear, realistic, and consistently met

 - Partner with leadership to translate creative direction into actionable production plans

Internal Coordination

 - Work directly with directors, editors, and designers to maintain workflow and priorities

 - Facilitate smooth handoffs between sales, creative, and production

 - Communicate progress, blockers, and needs clearly to the broader team




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 2+ years of experience in production, agency, or creative project management

 - Proven ability to manage complex projects with multiple deliverables

 - Excellent communication and organizational skills

 - Calm, proactive approach under pressure

 - Strong attention to detail and accountability to deadlines

 - Familiarity with Asana, Airtable, or similar project tools






BONUS POINTS

 - Familiarity with video production workflows

 - Background in creative agencies, studios, or startups

 - Experience with tools like Asana, Airtable, or similar project management platforms






WHY JOIN US

 - Be part of a small, fast-growing creative studio trusted by world-class startups and investors

 - Collaborate with tech founders to help launch innovative products, brands, and stories into the world

 - Work directly with the partners and production leadership, gaining exposure to both creative and business operations

 - Remote-friendly, flexible, and entrepreneurial environment

 - Healthcare coverage, PTO, and company holidays

 - $70,000-$90,000

source_jobs.title: Associate Producer
source_jobs.description:
We’re currently only reviewing SF-based applicants.

Please submit your application through this posting only. We’re unable to review applications sent via email, direct message, or website inquiry.



About Nen Creative

Nen Creative partners with tech startups to launch bold ideas into the world, creating the brands, products, and stories that fuel their growth.

Since 2022, we’ve partnered with some of the world’s top founders and institutions, including Amazon, Pear VC, Andreessen Horowitz, Mercor, Context Inc, Netic, Listen Labs, Tech Week, the University of Pittsburgh, Somethings, Mantis VC, Spur, Flora, and more.

We’re growing fast and looking for great people to join us.



About the Opportunity

We’re looking for a motivated, detail-oriented Associate Producer to join our growing team. You’ll work closely with our producers and creative leads, helping manage the day-to-day logistics of multiple productions at once.

You’ll be involved from pre-production through wrap, helping keep shoots organized and moving on track.

Location: Bay Area (required). This role will include in-person shoot days.



Responsibilities

 - Build and manage production budgets, track costs throughout each project, and flag risks or overages early

source_jobs.title: Post Production Supervisor
source_jobs.description:
Please submit your application through this posting only. We’re unable to review applications or follow-ups sent via email, direct message, or website inquiry.



About Nen Creative

Nen Creative partners with tech startups to launch bold ideas into the world, creating the brands, products, and stories that fuel their growth.

Since 2022, we’ve partnered with some of the world’s top founders and institutions, including Amazon, Pear VC, Crosby, Andreessen Horowitz, Wispr Flow, Rogo, Mercor, Rox, Netic, Listen Labs, Tech Week, Paraform, Spur, Flora, and more.

We’re growing fast and looking for great people to join us.



About the Opportunity

We’re looking for a detail-oriented Production Supervisor to own and elevate our post-production process across all projects.

This role sits at the center of our editing and motion pipeline. You’ll work closely with editors, motion designers, composers, and creative leads to ensure every piece of work meets a high bar for quality, clarity, and craft.

You’ll be responsible for managing timelines, implementing feedback, and acting as the final layer of QA across all deliverables. This includes stepping in on complex edits, guiding color across projects, and ensuring consistency in finishing.

You’ll also help manage external post-production partners and maintain our file systems and project infrastructure.



Responsibilities

 - Own post-production timelines across multiple projects, ensuring deadlines are met without compromising quality

 - Act as the final layer of QA across all edits, motion, sound, and finishing before delivery

 - Lead and implement color across projects, ensuring a consistent and intentional look and feel

 - Review cuts and provide clear, actionable feedback to editors and motion designers

 - Step in to handle complex feedback or tricky edits when needed

 - Manage external post-production collaborators, including editors, motion designers, composers, and sound designers

 - Ensure smooth handoff between internal and external teams, maintaining clarity and momentum

 - Oversee file organization, versioning, and delivery across cloud storage systems

 - Maintain and improve our project repository, storage, and archival systems

 - Coordinate final delivery of assets, ensuring all formats, specs, and versions are correct
   
   

What We’re Looking For

 - Based in the Bay Area (preferred)

 - 3+ years of experience in post-production, editing, or finishing roles

 - Strong editorial judgment and a sharp eye for quality, pacing, and storytelling

 - Experience giving clear, actionable feedback to editors and post teams

 - Exceptional excellence in color workflows and a strong understanding of color grading. Please refer to the body of work on our website portfolio for the standard bar (we'd like to raise it further with your help!).

 - Experience managing multiple projects and timelines simultaneously

 - Familiarity with motion graphics and sound workflows (you don’t need to execute everything, but you should be able to direct it)

 - Strong organizational skills and experience managing files, assets, and cloud-based workflows

 - Calm, detail-oriented, and dependable under pressure

 - A clear communicator who can work across creative and production teams

 - A strong sense of ownership and follow-through



Why Join Nen

 - Be part of a small, fast-growing creative studio trusted by world-class startups and investors

 - Work directly with partners and creative leadership on high-impact, visible projects

 - Help define and scale the post-production quality bar at Nen

 - Medical, dental, and vision coverage, 401(k), PTO, and company holidays

 - Salary range: $80,000 to $100,000, depending on experience, skills, and qualifications.

source_jobs.title: Editor
source_jobs.description:
Please submit your application through this posting only. We're unable to review applications sent via email, direct message, or website inquiry. SF (priority), NY, and LA-based applicants preferred.



About Nen Creative
Nen Creative partners with tech startups to bring bold ideas to life, creating the brands, products, and stories that fuel their growth. Since 2022, we've partnered with some of the world's top founders and institutions, including Amazon, Pear VC, Andreessen Horowitz, Mercor, Context Inc, Pylon, Listen Labs, Tech Week, Console, Mantis VC, Daydream, Flora, and more. We're growing fast and looking for great people to join us.



About the Opportunity
We're looking for a sharp, story-driven Editor to join our growing team. You'll cut a wide range of work across our roster of tech startup clients, from launch films and brand stories to product videos, social cutdowns, and event recaps. You'll partner closely with our directors and creative leads to take footage from raw to finished and shape the pacing, tone, and feel of each piece. You'll own the cut from selects through final delivery, working across multiple projects at any given time.



Responsibilities

 - Edit a wide variety of video content, primarily launch films/commercials, as well as product videos, testimonials, mini-docs, and social cutdowns.

 - Translate creative direction and scripts into compelling cuts that hit the intended tone, pacing, and story

 - Partner closely with directors and creative leads from selects through picture lock, iterating quickly on feedback

 - Handle sound design and basic mix work, including music selection, SFX, and dialogue cleanup

 - Color grade footage to match the look and feel of each project.

 - Create simple motion graphics, lower thirds, titles, and basic VFX as needed

 - Organize and maintain project files, media, and version control across multiple active projects

 - Manage your own timelines and flag risks early when scope, footage, or schedule shifts

 - Support delivery and asset prep for final handoff, including different cuts, aspect ratios, and platform specs

 - Stay current on editing trends, techniques, and tools that can elevate our work

What We're Looking For

 - Based in the Bay Area (preferred)

 - 3 to 5 years of experience preferred as an editor, ideally on commercial, brand, or documentary work

 - Strong reel that demonstrates story sensibility, pacing, and range across styles

 - Expert in Premiere Pro; comfortable in After Effects, DaVinci Resolve, or similar tools

 - Solid eye for color and ear for sound design and music

 - Highly organized with strong attention to detail and clean project management habits

 - Calm, proactive, and dependable on tight turnarounds

 - A clear communicator who takes feedback well and partners closely with directors

 - Takes ownership of the cut and pushes the work forward, not just executing notes

Why Join Nen

 - Be part of a small, fast-growing creative studio trusted by world-class startups and investors

 - Work directly with the partners and creative leadership on ambitious, high-impact projects

 - Cut a wide variety of work across founder-led brands, VC firms, and major tech companies

 - Medical, dental, and vision coverage, 401(k), PTO, and company holidays

 - Salary range $65,000 to $100,000

source_jobs.title: Cinematographer
source_jobs.description:
Please submit your application through this posting only. We’re unable to review applications or follow-ups sent via email, direct message, or website inquiry.



About Nen Creative

Nen Creative partners with tech startups to launch bold ideas into the world, creating the brands, products, and stories that fuel their growth.

Since 2022, we’ve partnered with some of the world’s top founders and institutions, including Amazon, Pear VC, Crosby, Andreessen Horowitz, Wispr Flow, Rogo, Mercor, Rox, Netic, Listen Labs, Tech Week, Paraform, Spur, Flora, and more.

We’re growing fast and looking for great people to join us.



About the Opportunity

We’re looking for a strong, technically sharp cinematographer to join our team in the Bay Area.

This role is for someone who deeply cares about the craft of cinematography, not just making an image “look good,” but understanding why it works. You should be comfortable owning camera, lighting, and grip setups across a wide range of productions, from scrappy founder interviews to polished commercials and brand films.

You’ll work closely with our directors, producers, and creative team to shape the visual approach for each project, execute on set, and help manage footage through post-production, including DaVinci Resolve project setup, color grading, and final exports when needed.

This is a hands-on role in a fast-moving agency environment. We need someone who is highly capable on set, adaptable, detail-obsessed, and willing to push the image as far as possible within real-world constraints.



What you'll do

 - Collaborate with directors and producers to develop the visual approach for each project and execute it on set

 - Own / capable of owning camera, lighting, and grip setup across a range of productions: commercials, brand films, talking heads, interviews, and more

 - Work within real-world budget constraints, get creative with what’s available, know where to spend and where to save, and deliver results that punch above their weight

 - Offload footage, build DaVinci Resolve projects with correct settings, and keep projects organized from shoot through post

 - Color grade projects and handle final exports from raw footage as needed

 - Adapt to our workflow and pipeline, while helping improve it over time

 - Wear different hats from project to project, depending on the needs of the production



What We’re Looking For

 - Based in the Bay Area, ideally close to San Francisco. You should expect to be on set in the SF area 3–4 days per week

 - Strong technical foundation in camera systems, lighting, and grip. You understand the tools deeply, not just how to use them

 - Genuine passion for the craft of cinematography. You care about the things most people don’t notice: how exposure index shifts dynamic range latitude, why one lens renders bokeh differently than another, how different coatings change the character of an image, and what gives a particular set of glass its falloff

 - Bring relentless attention to detail and never settle. If there’s time and room to improve a setup, you improve it, whether that means adding an edge light, using a flag to clean up a background, or making a subtle tweak to the key.

 - A make-work mentality. We often operate within real constraints and need someone who can innovate, problem-solve, and deliver premium results without relying on unlimited budget

 - Equal care for a talking head and a commercial. You bring the same level of taste, attention, and standard to every shoot, regardless of format or budget

 - Willingness to adopt and adapt to our creative taste, quality standard, and production workflow

 - Color grading and editing experience in DaVinci Resolve

 - Prior cinematography experience required. We can help sharpen your skills, but we need a strong technical baseline and someone who learns quickly

 - Comfortable working in an agency environment where timelines can shift, and projects sometimes require after-hours responsiveness

source_jobs.title: Motion Design Lead
source_jobs.description:
Please submit your application through this posting only. We're unable to review applications or follow-ups sent via email, direct message, or website inquiry.




ABOUT NEN CREATIVE

Nen Creative partners with tech startups to launch bold ideas into the world, creating the brands, products, and stories that fuel their growth.

Since 2022, we've partnered with some of the world's top founders and institutions, including Amazon, Pear VC, Andreessen Horowitz, Mercor, Context Inc, Netic, Listen Labs, Tech Week, Mantis VC, Spur, Flora, and more.

We're growing fast and looking for great people to join us.




ABOUT THE OPPORTUNITY

We're looking for a Motion Design Lead to help us scale the motion practice at Nen.

The work spans launch videos, commercials, product explainers, social content, and brand campaigns for our roster of tech clients. You'll work across 2D, UI, typography, compositing, and edit-driven animation, owning hands-on craft on the most ambitious projects while also guiding the rest of the motion team.

You'll partner closely with our producers, editors, designers, and directors to bring projects from concept to final delivery. Some weeks you'll be deep in a flagship animation yourself; others you'll be running critique, refining a teammate's cut, or building the templates and systems the team will use on the next ten projects.

This is a hands-on role in a fast-moving studio. We need someone with strong taste, real range, and the experience to hold a high creative bar across a team while still shipping their own work at the highest level.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Lead motion design across a range of client projects including launch videos, commercials, product explainers, social content, and brand campaigns

 - Create high quality motion work from concept through final delivery across 2D, UI, typography, compositing, and edit-driven animation

 - Help define the visual direction, pacing, transitions, and motion systems for projects

 - Work closely with producers, editors, designers, and directors to bring ideas to life

 - Review and provide feedback to other motion designers to maintain a high creative standard across projects

 - Help guide and mentor the motion team through critiques, collaboration, and workflow support

 - Build scalable templates, systems, and processes that improve consistency and efficiency across projects

 - Balance speed and quality while working across multiple projects and deadlines

 - Push work beyond the obvious. We value people with strong taste, attention to detail, and a desire to refine until the work feels exceptional

 - Help improve the overall motion pipeline, organization, and creative process as the team scales


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 7+ years of experience in motion design, ideally across both product/UI motion and brand or marketing work

 - Track record of leading or mentoring other motion designers

 - A strong reel demonstrating range across launch films, brand work, product motion, and social

 - Expert in After Effects and Figma

 - Fluency in at least one 3D tool (Cinema 4D, Blender, Spline, or similar)

 - Real visual design fundamentals — type, layout, color, composition, timing

 - Strong opinions about easing, timing, and restraint, and the ability to defend them in a room of smart people

 - Comfortable as both an IC and a leader. Can ship the hardest sequence yourself and unblock a teammate in the same week

 - Experience in studio, agency, or fast-moving startup environments

 - Self-directed and remote-ready. Can run a critique on Zoom, give clear written feedback in Figma, and keep a team aligned across time zones

 - Calm, organized, and dependable under pressure
   
   


WHY JOIN NEN

 - Be part of a small, fast-growing creative studio trusted by world-class startups and investors

 - Work directly with the partners and creative leadership on ambitious, high-impact projects

 - Define and scale the motion practice as we build the team out

 - Medi

source_jobs.title: Motion Designer
source_jobs.description:
Please submit your application through this posting only. We're unable to review applications or follow-ups sent via email, direct message, or website inquiry.





ABOUT NEN CREATIVE

Nen Creative partners with tech startups to launch bold ideas into the world, creating the brands, products, and stories that fuel their growth.

Since 2022, we've partnered with some of the world's top founders and institutions, including Amazon, Pear VC, Andreessen Horowitz, Mercor, Context Inc, Netic, Listen Labs, Tech Week, Mantis VC, Spur, Flora, and more.

We're growing fast and looking for great people to join us.




ABOUT THE OPPORTUNITY

We're looking for a Motion Designer to join our growing team and help us bring our clients' biggest ideas to life in motion.

You'll work on launch videos, commercials, product explainers, social content, and brand campaigns for our roster of tech clients. The work spans 2D, UI, typography, compositing, and edit-driven animation — sometimes a polished hero sequence for a product launch, sometimes a fast-turn social cutdown, often both in the same week.

You'll partner closely with our producers, editors, designers, and directors, taking creative direction from our Motion Design Lead and contributing your own ideas as projects develop. You'll own your work from concept through final delivery, iterate quickly on feedback, and bring real craft to every frame regardless of timeline or scope.

This is a hands-on role in a fast-moving studio. We need someone with strong fundamentals, sharp visual instincts, and the appetite to push their work past the obvious version.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design and animate motion work across launch videos, commercials, product explainers, social content, and brand campaigns

 - Create work from concept through final delivery across 2D, UI, typography, compositing, and edit-driven animation

 - Translate creative direction and storyboards into compelling motion that hits the intended tone, pacing, and feel

 - Work closely with producers, editors, designers, and directors to bring ideas to life

 - Iterate quickly on feedback from creative leads and clients without losing the craft

 - Help build and maintain templates, presets, and motion systems that improve consistency across projects

 - Balance speed and quality while working across multiple projects and deadlines

 - Push work beyond the obvious. We value people with strong taste, attention to detail, and a desire to refine until the work feels exceptional

 - Stay current on tools, techniques, and trends that can elevate the motion work we ship
   
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 3 to 5 years of experience as a motion designer, ideally in a studio, agency, or in-house creative team

 - A strong reel that demonstrates range across product and UI motion, brand work, and edit-driven animation

 - Expert in After Effects; comfortable in Figma and Premiere Pro or DaVinci Resolve

 - Strong visual design fundamentals — type, layout, color, composition, timing

 - Real point of view on motion — easing, transitions, restraint — and the ability to talk through your decisions

 - Solid eye for detail. You notice the things most people don't and care about getting them right

 - Comfortable taking feedback and iterating quickly without losing momentum

 - Experience in studio, agency, or fast-moving startup environments

 - Self-directed and remote-ready. You can manage your own workload, communicate clearly in writing, and stay aligned across time zones

 - Calm, organized, and dependable under pressure
   
   


WHY JOIN NEN

 - Be part of a small, fast-growing creative studio trusted by world-class startups and investors

 - Work directly with the partners and creative leadership on ambitious, high-impact projects

 - Define and scale the motion practice as we build the team out

 - Medical, dental, and vision coverage, 401(k), PTO, and company holidays

source_jobs.title: Brand Strategist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT NEN CREATIVE

Nen Creative partners with tech startups to launch bold ideas into the world, creating the brands, products, and stories that fuel their growth.

Since 2022, we've partnered with some of the world's top founders and institutions, including Amazon, Pear VC, Andreessen Horowitz, Mercor, Context Inc, Netic, Listen Labs, Tech Week, Mantis VC, Spur, Flora, and more.

We're growing fast and looking for great people to join us.




ABOUT THE OPPORTUNITY

We're looking for a Brand Strategist to lead the strategy practice at Nen.

The work centers on brand strategy for our branding engagements — positioning, narrative, audience, messaging, and the underlying point of view that gives a company its voice in the world. From there, the strategy extends into the marketing and copywriting that brings each brand to life across websites, decks, launch campaigns, and the various touchpoints our clients ship. You'll lead client engagements end-to-end, from discovery and research through final execution, working with founders who are often figuring out what they stand for and how to talk about it in public for the first time.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Lead brand strategy engagements for our branding clients — positioning, narrative, audience, messaging frameworks, brand voice, and identity briefs

 - Run discovery and research to get to a strategy: founder interviews, audience research, competitive landscape, brand audits, and stakeholder workshops

 - Develop marketing strategy for clients across launch campaigns, content programs, channel mix, and go-to-market storytelling, building on the brand foundation

 - Write copy across websites, decks, brand books, launch materials, and other touchpoints — landing pages, manifestos, product narratives, taglines, and short-form social

 - Brief and partner with our brand designers and creative leads so that strategy translates cleanly into identity, design, and the rest of the work we ship

 - Partner with our producers, motion team, and editors to make sure brand language and voice carry through every output

 - Help scope strategy work inside larger branding and production engagements, balancing depth with feasibility

 - Support new business when strategy is part of the pitch, helping shape opportunities from inquiry through close

 - Push the work beyond the obvious. We value people with strong taste, sharp thinking, and the appetite to refine a positioning line until it actually lands
   
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 4 to 7 years of experience in brand strategy, ideally at a brand or marketing consultancy, strategy-led studio, or in-house at a fast-moving company

 - Strong portfolio of brand strategy work — positioning, narrative, messaging frameworks, brand voice, and the briefs that bridge strategy into identity

 - Deep understanding of how brand strategy translates into identity, voice, and design, and how to brief and partner with designers to bring it to life

 - Strong copywriter across long-form and short-form, with writing samples that demonstrate range across narrative, web, decks, and brand

 - Marketing strategy fundamentals — audience, positioning, gtm, channels, funnel, and how brand strategy connects to launch, content, and performance

 - Strong research instincts. You know how to design discovery for a new client, ask the right questions in a founder interview, and pull insights from a messy landscape into a clear point of view

 - Confident client-facing presence. You can lead a workshop with a founder and a leadership team, hold the room, and push back when it matters

 - Comfortable working in a fast-moving agency environment where timelines shift, scopes evolve, and projects sometimes require after-hours responsiveness

 - Self-directed and remote-ready. You can run your own discovery, manage your own timelines, and keep clients aligned without constant supervision

 - Calm, organized, and dependable under pressure, with the ability to keep multiple projec

source_jobs.title: Creative Recruiter (Part Time)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT NEN CREATIVE

Nen Creative partners with tech startups to launch bold ideas into the world, creating the brands, products, and stories that fuel their growth.

Since 2022, we've partnered with some of the world's top founders and institutions, including Amazon, Pear VC, Andreessen Horowitz, Mercor, Context Inc, Netic, Listen Labs, Tech Week, Mantis VC, Spur, Flora, and more.

We're growing fast and looking for great people to join us.




ABOUT THE OPPORTUNITY

Nen Creative is looking for a part-time Creative Recruiter to help us identify, source, and screen top creative talent across video production, motion, design, and creative strategy.

This role is ideal for someone who has strong taste, understands creative work deeply, and knows how to identify high-potential talent beyond just resumes. You’ll work closely with our leadership and production team to help scale the company by building a stronger hiring pipeline across a range of creative disciplines.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Source and identify creative talent across production, animations, cinematography, editing, design, and other creative roles

 - Review portfolios, reels, resumes, and past work to help identify strong candidates

 - Conduct initial outreach, screenings, and introductory interviews

 - Help organize and improve our hiring process, candidate pipeline, and recruiting systems

 - Work closely with leadership to understand hiring needs, skill gaps, and role requirements

 - Coordinate interviews, feedback, and candidate communication

 - Help proactively build relationships with high-quality creative talent before hiring needs become urgent

 - Assist with writing and refining job postings and recruiting materials

 - Support both freelance/contract and full-time hiring efforts
   
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Strong understanding of creative industries, including video production, motion design, branding, or digital design

 - Strong creative judgment and ability to evaluate portfolios and reels

 - Excellent communication and organizational skills

 - Comfortable speaking with creative talent and assessing fit beyond technical skill alone

 - Experience recruiting for creative, agency, startup, or production environments preferred

 - Self-directed and able to operate independently

 - Comfortable working in a fast-moving, evolving environment

 - Strong attention to detail and follow-through
   
   


WHY JOIN NEN

 - Be part of a small, fast-growing creative studio trusted by world-class startups and investors

 - Work directly with the partners on ambitious, high-impact projects across some of the most interesting companies in tech

 - Lead and define the strategy practice at Nen, with real ownership over how we approach the work and how the team grows

 - Help shape the brands, narratives, and stories of the next generation of tech companies

source_jobs.title: Cinematographer - Relocation to San Francisco
source_jobs.description:
This is a full-time role based in San Francisco. We’re open to candidates currently based in Los Angeles or other production markets who are excited about relocating and becoming part of the team long-term. Relocation support may be available for the right candidate.



Please submit your application through this posting only. We’re unable to review applications or follow-ups sent via email, direct message, or website inquiry.



About Nen Creative

Nen Creative partners with tech startups to launch bold ideas into the world, creating the brands, products, and stories that fuel their growth.

Since 2022, we’ve partnered with some of the world’s top founders and institutions, including Amazon, Pear VC, Crosby, Andreessen Horowitz, Wispr Flow, Rogo, Mercor, Rox, Netic, Listen Labs, Tech Week, Paraform, Spur, Flora, and more.

We’re growing fast and looking for great people to join us.



About the Opportunity

We’re looking for a strong, technically sharp cinematographer to join our team in the Bay Area.

This role is for someone who deeply cares about the craft of cinematography, not just making an image “look good,” but understanding why it works. You should be comfortable owning camera, lighting, and grip setups across a wide range of productions, from scrappy founder interviews to polished commercials and brand films.

You’ll work closely with our directors, producers, and creative team to shape the visual approach for each project, execute on set, and help manage footage through post-production, including DaVinci Resolve project setup, color grading, and final exports when needed.

This is a hands-on role in a fast-moving agency environment. We need someone who is highly capable on set, adaptable, detail-obsessed, and willing to push the image as far as possible within real-world constraints.



What you'll do

 - Collaborate with directors and producers to develop the visual approach for each project and execute it on set

 - Own / capable of owning camera, lighting, and grip setup across a range of productions: commercials, brand films, talking heads, interviews, and more

 - Work within real-world budget constraints, get creative with what’s available, know where to spend and where to save, and deliver results that punch above their weight

 - Offload footage, build DaVinci Resolve projects with correct settings, and keep projects organized from shoot through post

 - Color grade projects and handle final exports from raw footage as needed

 - Adapt to our workflow and pipeline, while helping improve it over time

 - Wear different hats from project to project, depending on the needs of the production



What We’re Looking For

 - Willing to relocate to the Bay Area and work on set in the SF area 3–4 days per week

 - Strong technical foundation in camera systems, lighting, and grip

 - Genuine passion for the craft of cinematography. You care about the things most people don’t notice: how exposure index shifts dynamic range latitude, why one lens renders bokeh differently than another, how different coatings change the character of an image, and what gives a particular set of glass its falloff

 - Relentless attention to detail and high standards on every setup

 - A make-it-work mentality within real-world budgets and constraints

 - Equal care for talking heads, interviews, commercials, and brand films

 - Willingness to adapt to our creative taste, quality standard, and production workflow

 - Color grading and editing experience in DaVinci Resolve

 - Prior cinematography experience required

 - Comfortable with agency timelines, shifting production needs, occasional travel, and occasional weekend or after-hours production work

 - Must have a car

 

Why Join Nen

 - Be part of a small, fast-growing creative studio trusted by world-class startups and investors

 - Work directly with partners and creative leadership on high-impact, visible projects

 - Help define and scale the post-production quality bar at Nen

 -

source_jobs.title: Operations Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT NEN CREATIVE

Nen Creative partners with tech startups to launch bold ideas into the world, creating the brands, products, and stories that fuel their growth.

Since 2022, we've partnered with some of the world's top founders and institutions, including Amazon, Pear VC, Andreessen Horowitz, Mercor, Context Inc, Netic, Listen Labs, Tech Week, Mantis VC, Spur, Flora, and more.

We're growing fast and looking for great people to join us.




ABOUT THE OPPORTUNITY

Nen Creative is looking for an Operations Coordinator to help support and improve the systems that keep the company running day-to-day.

This role is ideal for someone early in their career who is highly organized, operationally minded, detail-oriented, and excited to grow inside a fast-moving creative startup environment. You’ll work closely with leadership across operations, finance, production, and project management to help maintain internal systems, support billing and operational workflows, and improve how the company operates as we continue to scale.

A big part of this role is making sure the basics happen consistently: invoices go out on time, vendors get paid, expenses are tracked, hours are logged, and internal systems stay organized. In the first few months, the focus will be learning our systems and executing these workflows reliably. After 3–6 months, you’ll start identifying and implementing process improvements that make our operations cleaner, faster, and more scalable.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Own day-to-day invoice intake, payment tracking, vendor follow-up, and reimbursement coordination

 - Support client invoicing, A/R follow-up, collections tracking, and billing coordination

 - Make sure invoices are sent on time and payment status is clearly tracked

 - Help ensure vendors are paid on time and approval workflows move quickly

 - Track team hours, follow up on missing time entries, and help reconcile hours against client agreements

 - Maintain clean records across Ramp, Gusto, QuickBooks, Monday, and internal tracking systems

 - Support expense tracking, receipt collection, card management, and reimbursement workflows

 - Help document SOPs and improve repeatable finance and ops processes over time

 - Flag issues early when something is missing, delayed, inaccurate, or likely to create downstream problems
   
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 0–2 years of experience preferred

 - Strong internship experience welcomed

 - Extremely organized and detail-oriented

 - Strong operational follow-through and sense of accountability

 - Comfortable handling recurring operational tasks with consistency and accuracy

 - Strong written communication skills

 - Comfortable learning and working across systems and tools quickly

 - Able to manage multiple priorities without things slipping through the cracks

 - Proactive, resourceful, and willing to learn

 - Comfortable in a fast-moving startup or agency environment

 - High level of discretion and professionalism around sensitive company information
   
   


WHY JOIN NEN

 - Be part of a small, fast-growing creative studio trusted by world-class startups and investors

 - Work directly with the partners on ambitious, high-impact projects across some of the most interesting companies in tech

 - Lead and define the strategy practice at Nen, with real ownership over how we approach the work and how the team grows

 - Help shape the brands, narratives, and stories of the next generation of tech companies

 - Medical, dental, and vision coverage, 401(k), PTO, and company holidays

source_jobs.title: Video Director
source_jobs.description:
This is a full-time role based in San Francisco. We’re open to candidates currently based in Los Angeles or other production markets who are excited about relocating and becoming part of the team long-term.

Please submit your application through this posting only. We’re unable to review applications or follow-ups sent via email, direct message, or website inquiry.




ABOUT NEN CREATIVE

Nen Creative partners with tech startups to launch bold ideas into the world, creating the brands, products, and stories that fuel their growth.

Since 2022, we’ve partnered with some of the world’s top founders and institutions, including Amazon, Pear VC, Andreessen Horowitz, Wispr Flow, Rogo, Mercor, Rox, Netic, Listen Labs, Tech Week, Paraform, Spur, Flora, and more.

We’re growing fast and looking for great people to join us.




ABOUT THE OPPORTUNITY

We’re looking for a highly creative, execution-oriented Creative Director to help lead the next phase of video and storytelling at Nen.

This role sits at the intersection of creative direction, scripting, directing, and production. We need someone who can develop strong creative concepts, shape narratives, write and refine scripts, direct talent on set, and help elevate the overall quality and originality of the work.

You’ll work closely with founders, producers, cinematographers, editors, and motion designers across a wide range of projects including founder-led videos, launch films, commercials, brand campaigns, product storytelling, interviews, and experimental creative work.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Develop creative concepts, narratives, and treatments for video projects across a range of formats

 - Write, refine, and support scripts for founder-led videos, commercials, brand films, launch content, and interviews

 - Direct shoots on set, including talent direction, pacing, performance, framing, and creative execution

 - Collaborate with producers and cinematographers to define the visual and emotional direction of projects

 - Work closely with editors and motion designers through post-production to ensure creative cohesion and quality

 - Help elevate Nen’s overall storytelling, creative quality, and directing standards

 - Translate abstract ideas into clear, emotionally resonant creative concepts

 - Push for stronger work while balancing real-world production timelines and constraints

 - Participate in creative reviews, brainstorming, scripting, and internal development conversations

 - Adapt creative approaches depending on the client, audience, platform, and project goals
   
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - Strong directing and storytelling instincts

 - Excellent taste and understanding of visual storytelling, pacing, and emotional impact

 - Experience directing commercials, brand films, interviews, documentaries, or narrative work

 - Strong scripting, messaging, and narrative development ability

 - Comfortable presenting ideas and communicating directly with founders and clients

 - Ability to guide non-professional talent and founders on camera

 - Deep understanding of how cinematography, editing, motion, sound, and pacing work together creatively

 - Comfortable working in a fast-moving production environment with occasional travel and shoot schedules
   
   


WHY JOIN NEN

 - Be part of a small, fast-growing creative studio trusted by world-class startups and investors

 - Work directly with the partners on ambitious, high-impact projects across some of the most interesting companies in tech

 - Lead and define the strategy practice at Nen, with real ownership over how we approach the work and how the team grows

 - Help shape the brands, narratives, and stories of the next generation of tech companies

 - Medical, dental, and vision coverage, 401(k), PTO, and company holidays

 - Salary Range: $70,000-$120,000 depending on experience

notes: Nen Creative is a product-focused studio partnering with tech startups, with a strong emphasis on branding, design, and marketing.
